content.claim: Hungary exhibits significantly higher educational inequality than OECD average: 28.1% of students below proficiency Level 2 (vs. OECD 23.0%), 9.3% at Level 5 or above (vs. OECD 12.6%), and socioeconomic background explains 23.1% of performance variance (vs. OECD 14.8%).
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Cross-sectional data; cannot isolate tracking as sole cause of inequality
OECD aggregate conclusion that school choice can lead to disparities is interpretive, not a direct empirical decomposition
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
